user.title: Add a Room
user.description: Configure Predictive Heating for a room. Rooms sharing the same climate entity are automatically grouped into a heating zone.

data.heat_share: Fraction of boiler heat going to this room

data_description.climate_entity: The thermostat that controls this room. If multiple rooms use the same thermostat, they will be grouped into a zone automatically.
data_description.gas_meter_sensor: Cumulative gas meter in m³. The integration computes its derivative to get the actual heating power in watts — much more accurate than treating heating as on/off. Later this can be replaced by a heat-pump electricity meter × COP.
data_description.boiler_efficiency: Seasonal boiler efficiency. HR-107 condensing boilers are typically 0.95; older boilers closer to 0.85.
data_description.heat_share: If you configure multiple rooms that share one gas meter, split the heat between them. Use 1.0 if only this room is modelled.
data_description.gas_calorific_value_mj_m3: Energy content per m³ of gas. Dutch Groningen-gas is ~35.17 MJ/m³; other grids differ.
data_description.floor_area_m2: Used once, to seed initial heat-loss and thermal-mass estimates. Leave blank to use generic defaults — the model will learn from observations regardless.
data_description.building_type: Roughly how well-insulated this room is. Used only for the initial bootstrap; the EKF will adjust H and C as it learns.
data_description.schedule_entity: Point to a schedule.* entity — the room target temperature will follow its ON/OFF state automatically. You can fine-tune the temperatures in the room's options.

data.heat_share: Fraction of boiler heat going to this room
data.away_grace_min: Minutes before switching to Away when nobody home

data_description.room_name: Renaming a room keeps all learned thermal properties — only the displayed name changes.
data_description.max_setpoint_delta: How far the thermostat setpoint is allowed to drift above target. Small values (≤1 °C) preserve OpenTherm modulation so the heat pump / boiler runs at its efficient flow temperature. Default 1.0 °C.
data_description.nudge_step: How much to change the thermostat setpoint when a correction is needed. Defaults to 0.5 °C — small enough to keep OpenTherm happy but noticeable.
data_description.nudge_interval_min: Minimum minutes between setpoint adjustments. Gives the boiler time to react before the next nudge.
data_description.window_sensors: Binary sensors whose 'on' state means a window/door is open. The room auto-drops to a safety setpoint and the controller shuts off when any of these report open.
data_description.gas_meter_sensor: Cumulative gas meter entity. Derivative → watts of heat delivered. Leave blank to fall back to binary heating on/off.
data_description.heat_share: When multiple rooms share one boiler, split the boiler heat between them (total should ≈ 1.0).
data_description.schedule_entity: Leave blank to set the temperature manually. When set, the room target follows this schedule entity's preset (if the slot carries 'preset'/'preset_mode'/'mode') or falls back to the on/off temperatures below.
data_description.schedule_on_temp: Used only when the schedule slot does NOT specify a preset name. Defaults to the Comfort preset.
data_description.schedule_off_temp: Used only when the schedule slot does NOT specify a preset name. Defaults to the Eco preset.
data_description.weather_entity: HA weather.* entity. The pre-heat planner reads its hourly forecast to extend pre-heat time on cold nights.
data_description.person_entities: When every listed person is away past the grace period the room switches to Away. When anyone returns the previous preset is restored automatically.
data_description.away_grace_min: Prevents a momentary GPS/phone glitch from cooling the room down. Default 10 min.
data_description.comfort_ramp: gradual: target temp ramps linearly from low to high over the pre-heat window. instant: jumps to the high target at the start of the window.
data_description.control_mode: observe: never writes to the thermostat — the integration only monitors and predicts. follow: writes the scheduled preset temperature to the thermostat, plus a small pre-heat nudge when needed so the room reaches the target on time.
data_description.max_preheat_nudge: Upper bound on how far above the scheduled target the pre-heat planner is allowed to push the setpoint while ramping in. Keep small (≤0.5 °C) so the heat pump stays in its efficient modulating range. Set to 0 to disable pre-heat nudges.
data_description.floor_area_m2: Used as an initial estimate for heat loss (H) and thermal mass (C). Only applied when the model hasn't learned anything yet.
data_description.building_type: Insulation quality category. Only affects the initial estimate — the EKF takes over once it has observations.

couplings.title: Thermal couplings
couplings.description: Pick a neighbour to configure the partition between this room and it. The label shows the current U-values (closed/open, W/K) and whether the learner is on.

couple_edit.title: Edit coupling: {neighbour_name}
couple_edit.description: Configures the heat exchange between this room and {neighbour_name}. U-values are in W/K — closed solid door ≈ 10–20, glazed door ≈ 20–40, open doorway ≈ 60–150. Enable 'learn' to let the integration refine these values from observations. Changes are mirrored to {neighbour_name} automatically.

data.enabled: Include this coupling in the model

data_description.enabled: When off, the partition is declared but ignored in the thermal math — useful to temporarily disconnect a neighbour.
data_description.u_closed: Heat conductance while the door is closed. A solid interior door is ~10–20 W/K; a glazed door is 20–40; no door at all is closer to the 'open' value.
data_description.u_open: Heat conductance while the door is open. A normal doorway is 60–150 W/K.
data_description.door_sensor: Optional binary sensor reporting the door's open/closed state. Without a sensor the closed value is always used. Coupling values are mirrored to the neighbour automatically.
data_description.learn: Nudges u_closed / u_open toward the values that best explain the observed cross-room temperature differences. Uncheck to freeze them at the current value.
